example_prompt: Trigger a mobile money payment directly on the MTN channel for a customer in Cameroon.

reference.description: The transaction reference returned by create_payment (transaction.reference). Used as a path parameter.

channel.description: Slug of the payment channel to use, e.g. "cm.mtn" or "cm.orange".

data.description: Optional channel-specific payer data. Required in practice for mobile money channels (provide phone). Optional for hosted-redirect channels.

phone.description: Customer phone number in E.164 format, e.g. "+237670000000". Required for mobile money channels.

account_number.description: Account number for non-mobile-money channels (e.g. card last-four or bank account). Channel-dependent.

country.description: Optional ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code of the payer. Inferred from the channel when omitted.

client_ip.description: Optional IP address of the end-user initiating the payment. Used by Notch Pay for fraud screening; pass the request's `x-forwarded-for` value when available.

status.description: Payment status immediately after processing, typically "pending".

gotchas: process_payment must be called after create_payment — it requires the reference from the created transaction and will return 404 if the reference does not exist.

A 200 response with status "Accepted" means the payment was submitted to the mobile operator, not that it has been confirmed. Always use verify_payment afterwards to check the final status before fulfilling an order.
The phone number in data.phone must be in E.164 format (e.g. "+237670000000"). Passing a local format without the country code will cause the mobile operator to reject the request.
The channel slug is country-and-operator specific (e.g. "cm.mtn", "cm.orange"). Using a slug that does not match the customer's operator will result in a failed payment.
Calling process_payment on a transaction that is already 'complete', 'canceled', or 'failed' returns 422 with message 'Payment cannot be processed'.
The Authorization header uses your public key with no 'Bearer ' prefix.
The API also accepts PUT /payments/{reference} with the same body and response — this is a legacy alias. Prefer POST.
